title: Fitting a concealed euro hinge to a cabinet door
subtitle: Drill the 35 mm cup hole, fit the hinge cup and mounting plate, and adjust the hinge for correct door alignment
excerpt: How to fit concealed cup hinges to a cabinet door. Covers drilling the 35 mm Forstner cup hole to the correct depth, pressing in the hinge cup, positioning the mounting plate on the cabinet frame, and adjusting the three-way hinge movement.
sourceNotes: UK kitchen and cabinet-fitting trade practice. Blum and Häfele concealed hinge generic installation guidance (referenced principles, not brand instructions). Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual (referenced, not reproduced).

glossaryTerms.definition: A hinge in which a circular cup body presses into a 35 mm blind hole drilled in the door back face. A mounting plate screws to the cabinet side or frame and clips to the hinge arm. The hinge mechanism is hidden when the door is closed. Three adjustment screws allow the door to be moved in-out, left-right, and up-down after fitting (simplifying alignment on carcasses that are not perfectly square. Standard cup hole depth is 12 to 13 mm; the 35 mm Forstner bit is the universal bit for all makes.

content.text: fits into a 35 mm cup hole drilled in the door and clips to a mounting plate on the carcass. The three adjustment screws on the hinge arm mean the door can be moved in three directions after fitting) making alignment much easier than a surface-mounted hinge.

Mark the cup hole centre at 37 mm from the door edge for a full-overlay door (standard kitchen door), or 22 mm for a half-overlay or inset door (check the hinge specification. The holes sit 100 to 150 mm from the top and bottom of the door on a standard cabinet. Use the combination square to set these distances exactly and mark with a pencil cross.

Fit a 35 mm Forstner bit in the cordless drill and set a depth stop or tape marker at 12 to 13 mm. Drill the cup hole at 90 degrees to the door face) any tilt will mean the cup does not press flush. Work slowly; Forstner bits need a steady rate to cut cleanly without tear-out. Vacuum the waste from the hole.
Press in the hinge cup and fit the arm

Press the hinge cup into the hole (it should fit snugly without forcing. Drive the two small fixing screws through the hinge flanges into the door face. Clip the hinge arm to the cup body. The arm clicks into the cup on most modern hinges with a positive snap.
Fit the mounting plate and hang the door

Screw the mounting plate to the inside face of the carcass side panel, 37 mm from the front edge for full-overlay doors. Clip the hinge arm onto the mounting plate) it slides on and clicks or screws to lock. Adjust door position with the three adjustment screws on the hinge arm: the side screw moves the door in and out; the height screw moves it up and down; the depth screw moves it in and out. Adjust both hinges equally for a square close.
